_publications._publicationTitle: Modeling Tumor: Lymphatic Interactions in Lymphatic Metastasis of Triple Negative Breast Cancer
_publications._context: Novel microfluidic chambers (TAME) used to model spatiotemporal interactions between TNBC cells and lymphatic endothelial cells in 3D coculture; two configurations: separate wells (monoculture) and linked wells (parallel coculture with shared secretome)

_publications._publicationTitle: Fibroblasts Attenuate Anti-Tumor Drug Efficacy in Tumor Cells via Paracrine Interactions with Tumor Cells in 3D Plexiform Neurofibroma Cultures
_publications._context: We employed 3D cultures grown in the patented microfluidic culture devices called TAME (tissue architecture and microenvironment engineering; Patent# US10227556B2) chips [24] for all experiments

basicInfo.description: Patented microfluidic 3D culture devices (Tissue Architecture and Microenvironment Engineering; Patent US10227556B2) with separate-well (monoculture) and linked-well (parallel coculture) configurations enabling paracrine signaling studies; originally developed for TNBC/lymphatic coculture and applied to NF1 plexiform neurofibroma tumor-fibroblast drug resistance modeling
basicInfo.synonyms: TAME (tissue architecture and microenvironment engineering); TAME microfluidic chips
basicInfo.modelLimitations: Device requires contact from developer (patented); original validation performed in TNBC/lymphatic context; NF1 application limited to one published study; does not include axonal component
basicInfo.developerAffiliation: Department of Neurology, Henry Ford Health; Department of Pharmacology, Wayne State University
